title: Alchemical Reduction
description: Fully react a polymer by destroying adjacent units of the same type but opposite polarity, then find which unit type to remove to minimize the result.
writeup:
## Approach

The input is a single long polymer string. Adjacent units of the same letter but different case react and annihilate each other (`aA`, `Bb`, etc.). The process repeats until no more reactions are possible.

## Part 1

Simulate the reaction with a stack:

- Iterate through each unit in the polymer.
- If the top of the stack is the same letter but opposite case, pop it (reaction).
- Otherwise, push the unit onto the stack.
- The remaining stack is the fully reacted polymer.

This runs in O(n) — one pass through the string.

## Part 2

Find the unit type whose removal leads to the shortest fully reacted polymer:

- There are only 26 possible unit types.
- For each letter, strip all instances (both cases) from the original polymer, then run the O(n) reaction.
- Return the minimum length across all 26 candidates.

Total complexity: O(26n).

## Code Highlights

- The stack approach allows one pass, no repeated scanning.
- `stack[-1].lower() == unit.lower()` checks same type; `stack[-1] != unit` checks opposite polarity.
- Part 2 reuses the same `react()`.
- Visualizer animates the stack building in real time, then shows a bar chart of all 26 candidates for part 2.
